label: Al-Shabaab South-Central Corridor
notes: Principal Al-Shabaab movement corridor from Middle Jubba through Bay region to central Somalia; used for logistics, reinforcement, and tax collection

label: SNA Main Supply Route: Mogadishu–Baidoa
notes: Primary SNA logistics axis between capital and Southwest State; frequently targeted by Al-Shabaab IEDs and ambushes

label: SNA 2022 Offensive Axis: Mogadishu–Beledweyne
notes: Primary axis of advance for 2022 SNA/Macawisley Hiiraan offensive; route up Shabelle River valley

label: KDF Supply Corridor: Kenya Border–Kismayo
notes: Kenya Defence Forces logistics route from Kenyan border to Kismayo; critical for KDF ATMIS operations in Jubbaland

label: MV Ruen Mother-Ship Patrol Area (2023–24)
notes: Area of operations for pirate mother ship MV Ruen after hijacking Nov 2023; used for further attacks before Indian Navy retook vessel Mar 2024

label: Gulf of Aden International Shipping Lane
notes: Critical global shipping corridor; ~20,000 vessels/year; primary target of Somali piracy 2008-2012 and resurgence 2023-24

label: WFP Aid Corridor: Mogadishu–Bay Region
notes: World Food Programme and NGO aid supply route to drought-affected Bay and Bakool regions; negotiated access with armed actors required

label: Al-Shabaab Coastal Arms Smuggling Route
notes: Suspected arms smuggling route from Barawe coastal area toward Mogadishu hinterland; documented by UN Monitoring Group

label: SNA Axis: Kismayo–Baidoa
notes: SNA and KDF ATMIS operational axis connecting Jubbaland coast to Bay Region; key for coordinating southwest operations

label: IDP Displacement Flow: South Somalia–Mogadishu
notes: Primary displacement corridor; hundreds of thousands of IDPs fled conflict and drought in Lower Shabelle and Bay/Bakool toward Mogadishu settlements

label: ENDF Logistics: Ethiopia–Beledweyne Corridor
notes: Ethiopian military supply route from Ethiopia border through Hiiraan to Beledweyne; used by ENDF AMISOM/ATMIS contingent

label: Charcoal Export Route (UN Banned)
notes: Al-Shabaab charcoal export route via coastal dhows to Gulf states (UAE, Oman); banned under UNSC Resolution 2036 but documented violation by UN Monitoring Group

label: Maritime Supply: Mogadishu–Kismayo
notes: Maritime logistics route between Mogadishu and Kismayo; supplementary to overland routes and critical when roads are threatened by Al-Shabaab

label: EU NAVFOR Atalanta Patrol Zone
notes: EU NAVFOR counter-piracy patrol area covering Gulf of Aden and western Indian Ocean; WFP convoy escort missions also conducted within this zone

label: Al-Shabaab Kenya Border Infiltration
notes: Al-Shabaab cross-border movement corridor; used for attacks in Kenya's Northeast Province and to recruit Kenyan fighters (including for Westgate and Garissa attacks)

label: Operation Rolling Thunder / Hilaac — Lower Shabelle Axis
notes: Joint SNA and AUSSOM/UPDF Battle Group 44 ground offensive axis into Lower Shabelle launched Feb 28 – Mar 2, 2026. Operation Bader umbrella; sub-operation 'Hilaac' (Lightning) cleared Darasaalaam and Mubarak towns of al-Shabaab presence. Commanded by Brig. Gen. Jackson Kayanja. Bulunagaad joint operation (30+ killed) occurred along this corridor in late March 2026.

label: AFRICOM Airstrike: Al-Shabaab Near Xawaadleey (Mar 11, 2026)
notes: US Africa Command airstrike targeting al-Shabaab approximately 60 km north of Mogadishu near Xawaadleey. Conducted in coordination with the Federal Government of Somalia. Part of the intense 2026 airstrike campaign in Somalia (47+ strikes by mid-March). Origin shown from Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti.

label: AFRICOM Strikes: ISIS-Somalia, Golis Mountains (Mar 8/16/18, 2026)
notes: Multiple AFRICOM airstrikes targeting ISIS-Somalia in the Golis/Cal Miskaad Mountains, approximately 45–70 km southeast of Bossaso, Puntland. Strikes on March 8, 16, and 18, 2026 — authorized by Defense Secretary Hegseth at President Trump's direction. ISIS-Somalia operates from cave complexes in this mountain range. Origin shown from Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti.

label: AFRICOM Airstrike: Al-Shabaab NW of Kismayo (Mar 27, 2026)
notes: US Africa Command confirmed airstrike targeting Al-Shabaab approximately 132 km northwest of Kismayo (Jubbaland), conducted in coordination with the Federal Government of Somalia. Part of the intensified 2026 air campaign; US had conducted 43+ strikes in Somalia by early March 2026, pace tracking to exceed the 2025 record of 124 annual strikes. Origin shown from Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti. AFRICOM Press Release #36323.

label: SNA Offensive Axis: Mogadishu–Baidoa Seizure (Mar 30, 2026)
notes: SNA forces and allied 'South West Salvation' faction advanced along the Mogadishu–Baidoa corridor and seized Baidoa (Southwest State capital) on March 30, 2026, following the constitutional standoff with President Laftagareen. Ethiopian AUSSOM troops at Baidoa stood aside. SW State President Laftagareen resigned March 30. First federal military seizure of a state capital since independence. Significant civilian displacement (50,000+) reported by UNHCR.

label: SNA/Intl Airstrikes: Juba Regions Offensive (Apr 15–16, 2026)
notes: Coordinated airstrikes with 'international support' (consistent with AFRICOM-enabled operations) targeting Al-Shabaab in the Juba regions, Apr 15–16, 2026. Official toll: 54 Al-Shabaab fighters killed in airstrikes; additional 27 killed in ground operations by April 18. Target area: Middle Jubba/Jilib Al-Shabaab stronghold. Joint operation with Jubbaland forces. Origin shown from Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti.

label: SNA/Macawisley Counter-Offensive Axis: Beledweyne–Adan Yabal (Apr 21, 2026)
notes: Axis of planned SNA and Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal (seized by Al-Shabaab on April 19, 2026) from Beledweyne sector. President Mohamud ordered immediate operations on April 19; forces mobilizing from Beledweyne as of April 21. Approximately 100 Al-Shabaab fighters routed SNA from the town and surrounding outposts.

label: AFRICOM Strike: ISIS-Somalia SE of Bosaso (Apr 17, 2026) — Strike #56
notes: AFRICOM Press Release #36380 (announced April 19): Strike targeting ISIS-Somalia approximately 30 miles (48 km) SE of Bosaso, Puntland. The 56th US airstrike in Somalia in 2026 — an unprecedented pace (200+/year annualized). Targets ISIS-Somalia cave/mountain complexes in the Golis/Cal Miskaad range. Origin shown from Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti.
